next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 9

The lamb was overcooked.

Hermione noticed it the way she'd started noticing most things about Malfoy: sideways, while pretending her attention was on the work. Forty minutes in the kitchen had left the chops grey at the edges and a little dry in the middle. He set the plates down in the library without apology. She ate all of hers without comment.

Three weeks ago, eating overcooked lamb in Malfoy Manor would have registered as absurd. Tonight the absurdity was that it didn't.

Abelard Malfoy's letters lay spread between them beside a genealogical chart and two stacks of reference books the shelves had produced before either of them asked. Malfoy read while he ate. Ink darkened the tips of his fingers where he'd been annotating the originals, ever since she'd found the transcription error that turned "to anchor" into "to sever." Her attention kept catching on his hands.

Only after several minutes did she admit what she was doing. His thumb snagged on a page. He put it to his mouth for better grip, absent-minded, and heat moved through her low and fast. Looking down helped very little. Her plate held a bare bone, a smear of mint jelly, a knife at the wrong angle. None of it explained the shift under her ribs.

Since the examination that had gone wrong, or right, the Manor had turned almost accommodating. The route from the guest wing to the library stayed stable. Shelves surfaced the right texts. Fires lit when she entered and banked when she left. By increments so small they might have passed for coincidence, comfort had become part of the house.

"The third...
open_hooks_to_carry: The Mark memory flood: specific, sensory — Voldemort's study, sixteen years old, his father's hand, the pain that made him pass out. Hermione feels the fear and the age of the boy
The aftermath silence — not careful distance but the quiet of two people who have been inside the same pain

payoffs_to_land.description: The resonance that began as faint proximity-awareness in Ch5 now transmits full sensory memory — the bond's capacity has deepened

prev_tail_excerpt:
...timber and old pipes, a slower rhythm moved through stone and plaster, rising and falling as if the building had lungs hidden somewhere in the walls.

Her room stayed fixed. Bed. Bathroom down the corridor. Route to the library. Small mercies, if that was what they were.

Two floors above and to the east, Draco was awake.

For the past week and a half the resonance had lived at the edge of her attention like background noise. It no longer belonged there. Pacing registered as a drag behind her sternum. Stillness had weight. The Mark's active flares came through sharper than its baseline unease, and each examination made the whole signal cleaner instead of fainter.

Narcissa's warning altered the interpretation. What Hermione carried from his sleepless nights might be the house, or the Mark, or both together. It was still there. It was still getting stronger.

And it was measurable.

That was the part she disliked most. His worst nights ran past three. The Mark usually settled closer to four. She knew the difference now between a restless circuit of the room and the heavier stillness when he sat down and failed to sleep in place. None of that knowledge had been offered to her. She had it anyway.

At half past one the resonance sharpened. He was pacing again.

Hermione lay still for another count of ten, then got out of bed, crossed to the desk, and pulled her notebook from the drawer. Diagnostic Residue: Mark-Adjacent Sensory Artifacts stared up at her from the top of the page.

At 1:32 a.m. she crossed out Residue and began a record she wasn't sure she had the right to keep.
state_deltas_required: The Mark floods shared memory during an examination — Draco relives taking the Mark at sixteen, and Hermione feels it through the resonance
She has seen his worst moment through his own sensory experience. The clinical frame between examiner and subject is permanently broken
